title: Nephrodium effusum
intro: Nephrodium effusum, also known as Lady Fern, is a species of fern native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its delicate fronds and lacy foliage. Traditionally, Nephrodium effusum has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The bioactive constituents of Nephrodium effusum include flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Nephrodium effusum is often prepared as a tea or used in infusions, and its extracts are also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.

uses: Nephrodium effusum, also known as the Rigid Buckler Fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties.
